qualifying_org_definition: Non-profit sites that serve a useful public service such as health, social service, environmental protection, education, urban and rural development, welfare, recreation, public facilities, public safety, community service, services to aged or disabled citizens, and child care organizations. (Verbatim from the SNAP Community Service Volunteer/Workfare Verification form.)
qualifyingOrgShort: Any non-profit site serving a useful public-service purpose (health, social service, environmental protection, education, welfare, recreation, public safety, community service, services to aged/disabled, child care, etc.).
verification_instrument: ABAWD Volunteer Form (titled 'SNAP Community Service Volunteer/Workfare Verification - Action Required'); current fillable version 'ABAWD Volunteer Form Fillable 11.24.25.pdf'
verificationFormName: ABAWD Volunteer Form ("SNAP Community Service Volunteer/Workfare Verification")
hours_formula_detail: Required monthly hours = monthly household SNAP allotment divided by the higher of the state or federal minimum wage, rounded down to the nearest hour. Maine state minimum wage is $15.10/hr effective Jan 1, 2026 (state rate exceeds the $7.25 federal floor, so $15.10 controls). Rule 240A (Dec 2025, implementing HR1/OBBBA) codifies: 'dividing the SNAP household benefit allotment by state or federal minimum wage, whichever is higher, rounded down to the nearest hour,' with the example '$150 monthly benefit / $15/hour minimum wage = 10 hours per month maximum.' Representative figure: a 1-person household at the FY2026 max allotment ($298) yields floor(298/15.10)=19 hours/month. Hours scale with household benefit and may be met collectively across household members; no member may be required to work more than 30 hours/week (workfare/volunteer combined with other compensated work), and required hours never exceed the value of the allotment.
formulaDetail: monthly SNAP allotment / max($15.10 state, $7.25 federal) = $15.10, rounded down to nearest hour. Max-allotment 1-person: floor($298 / $15.10) = 19 hrs/mo.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Maine's qualifying-organization definition is mission-based only (non-profit serving a public-service purpose) with no geographic restriction on the org's location. Neither the ABAWD Volunteer Form, Rule 240A, nor DHHS guidance addresses in-state vs. out-of-state nonprofit location. Absent an explicit state restriction, the federal default (any qualifying nonprofit) should apply operationally.
geographic_waivers: Maine's prior geographic waiver (Rule 234A) covered 213 cities, towns, unorganized territories, townships, and reservations; approved by USDA-FNS on Sept 17, 2024, effective retroactive to Oct 1, 2024 through Sept 30, 2025. That waiver period EXPIRED Sept 30, 2025. Under HR1/OBBBA (2025) area-based waivers are restricted to areas with unemployment over 10%, a much narrower threshold. No confirmed active FY2026 (Oct 2025-Sep 2026) Maine geographic waiver was found on the USDA-FNS FY2025-2029 waiver page or Maine DHHS rulemaking pages as of June 2026. Maine is treated as enforcing ABAWD statewide for FY2026, with the caveat that USDA issued some waiver-reinstatement guidance in spring 2026 following litigation over early waiver terminations elsewhere; a narrow Maine high-unemployment waiver cannot be fully ruled out and should be re-checked.
geographicWaivers: Prior 213-locality waiver (Rule 234A) expired Sep 30, 2025. No confirmed active FY2026 geographic waiver found under OBBBA's >10% unemployment threshold. Treated as statewide enforcement, pending re-check due to spring-2026 federal waiver-reinstatement litigation.
submissionMechanism: Recipient (or certifying org) submits the completed/signed ABAWD Volunteer Form by mail/fax to the Farmington DHHS office (114 Corn Shop Lane, Farmington, ME 04938; Fax (207) 778-8429), by e-mail to Farmington.DHHS@Maine.gov, or by upload to the recipient's account at MyMaineConnection.gov.
submission_deadline: The form itself states volunteer hours 'will need to be verified at each application or annual eligibility review.' For ongoing monthly compliance, Maine SNAP change reporting follows the 10th-of-the-following-month convention. The form does not print a hard per-month deadline; the binding checkpoints are application and annual recertification.
submissionDeadline: Verified at each application and annual eligibility review (per the form). No hard per-month deadline printed on the form; monthly change-reporting convention is the 10th of the following month.
wage_caveat: Several Maine municipalities set higher LOCAL employment minimum wages (e.g., Portland $16.75/hr effective Jan 1, 2026; Rockland). These local rates govern employment law, NOT the SNAP ABAWD formula. Rule 240A and the ABAWD Volunteer Form direct recipients to the STATE minimum wage ($15.10), not local rates, so the formula is uniform statewide and regionalWages is empty.
conflictsWithPrior: remote_stance: changed 'unknown' -> 'silent' (more accurate: form is genuinely silent, not merely undetermined).
minimum-wage basis: prior cited only state $15.10; verified Rule 240A wording is 'state OR federal minimum wage, whichever is higher, rounded down' (state controls in ME, so no numeric change).
verification form name: normalized to 'ABAWD Volunteer Form' / 'SNAP Community Service Volunteer/Workfare Verification' (the prior file used the filename as the title).
submission_mechanism: added 'fax' (the form explicitly lists fax to (207) 778-8429 alongside mail/email/portal).
unresolved: Whether remote/virtual volunteering counts toward ABAWD hours — no Maine source addresses it (form and Rule 240A are silent).
Whether an out-of-state 501(c)(3) may certify hours — no Maine source addresses it; inferred allowed by federal default but not confirmed.
Exact active FY2026 geographic-waiver footprint, if any, given spring-2026 federal waiver-reinstatement litigation; no confirmed active Maine waiver located.
Whether any hard monthly submission deadline exists beyond application/recert checkpoints and the general 10th-of-month change-reporting convention.
notes: VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Confirmed Maine is a clean workfare_formula state with a general nonprofit volunteer lane any recipient can self-initiate at any qualifying nonprofit (no provider pre-registration, MOU, county assignment, EDP, or FSET enrollment required as a prestep). Direct primary-source confirmations this pass: (1) ABAWD Volunteer Form body text (extracted from the official fillable PDF) states verbatim: 'To calculate their required hours, recipients can divide their monthly household benefit by the State minimum wage available at maine.gov/labor/labor_laws/minimumwagefaq/ to determine the number of hours necessary' and 'Approved work sites may be non-profit sites that serve a useful public service such as health, social service, environmental protection, education, ... and child care organizations.' (2) Submission methods quoted verbatim from the form: 'Recipients must send this form to the address or fax above, e-mail it to Farmington.DHHS@Maine.gov or upload it to their account at MyMaineConnection.gov.' (3) Reverification cadence quoted: 'Volunteer hours will need to be verified at each application or annual eligibility review.' (4) Maine DHHS applications-forms page lists exactly two ABAWD forms (the Volunteer Form and a Medical/Behavioral Health exemption form) plus a Community College verification form — confirming no separate work-registration/enrollment form exists. (5) SNAP Rule 240A (Dec 10, 2025, implementing HR1/OBBBA) codifies the formula: 'dividing the SNAP household benefit allotment by state or federal minimum wage, whichever is higher, rounded down to the nearest hour,' caps required hours at the value of the allotment, limits combined work to 30 hrs/week, and allows household members to collectively meet the hours; it adds NO new procedural gate on the volunteer lane. (6) Maine state minimum wage = $15.10/hr effective Jan 1, 2026 (Maine DOL); state exceeds the $7.25 federal floor, so $15.10 controls. (7) FY2026 1-person max allotment = $298 (USDA-FNS COLA FY26); floor($298/$15.10)=19 hrs/mo, matching the prior representative figure. WAGE REGIONALITY: Portland ME ($16.75) and other municipalities set higher LOCAL employment minimum wages, but the ABAWD form and Rule 240A point to the STATE minimum wage, so the formula is uniform statewide; regionalWages left empty and countyVariation=false. WAIVERS: prior 213-locality waiver (Rule 234A) expired Sept 30, 2025; under OBBBA's >10% unemployment threshold no confirmed active FY2026 Maine geographic waiver was located; enforcement treated as statewide for FY2026 with a noted caveat about spring-2026 federal waiver-reinstatement litigation. remote_stance and out-of-state-nonprofit remain genuinely silent in all Maine sources. Confidence: HIGH — classification, hours formula, and form name are each directly confirmed by primary state sources (the form PDF and Rule 240A).
